name: foxglove-schemas-flatbuffer
documentation:
# Foxglove Schemas (Flatbuffer)

This package provides [Flatbuffer](https://google.github.io/flatbuffers/) classes for [Foxglove Schemas](https://docs.foxglove.dev/docs/visualization/message-schemas/introduction).

## Installation

Install via [Pipenv](https://pipenv.pypa.io/en/latest/) by adding `foxglove-schemas-flatbuffer` to your `Pipfile` or via the command line:

```bash
pipenv install foxglove-schemas-flatbuffer
```

## Usage

Import types from the `foxglove_schemas_flatbuffer` module as follows:

```py
import flatbuffers
import foxglove_schemas_flatbuffer.CompressedImage as CompressedImage
from foxglove_schemas_flatbuffer import get_schema

builder = flatbuffers.Builder(1024)
png = builder.CreateString("png")
CompressedImage.Start(builder)
CompressedImage.AddFormat(builder, png)
img = CompressedImage.End(builder)
builder.Finish(img)

# Serialized flatbuffer schema
schema_data = get_schema("CompressedImage")

# Serialized CompressedImage message
msg_data = builder.Output()
```

## Stay in touch

Join our [Slack channel](https://foxglove.dev/slack) to ask questions, share feedback, and stay up to date on what our team is working on.

modules.name: foxglove_schemas_flatbuffer.CameraCalibration
documentation: automatically generated by the FlatBuffers compiler, do not modify

methods.name: foxglove_schemas_flatbuffer.CameraCalibration.CameraCalibration.GetRootAsCameraCalibration
documentation: This method is deprecated. Please switch to GetRootAs.

methods.name: foxglove_schemas_flatbuffer.CameraCalibration.CameraCalibration.FrameId
documentation: Frame of reference for the camera. The origin of the frame is the optical center of the camera. +x points to the right in the image, +y points down, and +z points into the plane of the image.

methods.name: foxglove_schemas_flatbuffer.CameraCalibration.CameraCalibration.DistortionModel
documentation: Supported parameters: `plumb_bob` (k1, k2, p1, p2, k3) and `rational_polynomial` (k1, k2, p1, p2, k3, k4, k5, k6). Distortion models are based on [OpenCV's](https://docs.opencv.org/2.4/modules/calib3d/doc/camera_calibration_and_3d_reconstruction.html) [pinhole camera model](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Distortion_%28optics%29#Software_correction). This is the same [implementation used by ROS](http://docs.ros.org/en/diamondback/api/image_geometry/html/c++/pinhole__camera__model_8cpp_source.html)

methods.name: foxglove_schemas_flatbuffer.CameraCalibration.CameraCalibration.K
documentation: A 3x3 row-major matrix for the raw (distorted) image.
Projects 3D points in the camera coordinate frame to 2D pixel coordinates using the focal lengths (fx, fy) and principal point (cx, cy).

methods.name: foxglove_schemas_flatbuffer.CameraCalibration.CameraCalibration.R
documentation: Rectification matrix (stereo cameras only, 3x3 row-major matrix)
A rotation matrix aligning the camera coordinate system to the ideal stereo image plane so that epipolar lines in both stereo images are parallel.

methods.name: foxglove_schemas_flatbuffer.CameraCalibration.CameraCalibration.P
documentation: By convention, this matrix specifies the intrinsic (camera) matrix of the processed (rectified) image. That is, the left 3x3 portion is the normal camera intrinsic matrix for the rectified image.
It projects 3D points in the camera coordinate frame to 2D pixel coordinates using the focal lengths (fx', fy') and principal point (cx', cy') - these may differ from the values in K.
For monocular cameras, Tx = Ty = 0. Normally, monocular cameras will also have R = the identity and P[1:3,1:3] = K.

For a stereo pair, the fourth column [Tx Ty 0]' is related to the position of the optical center of the second camera in the first camera's frame. We assume Tz = 0 so both cameras are in the same stereo image plane. The first camera always has Tx = Ty = 0. For the right (second) camera of a horizontal stereo pair, Ty = 0 and Tx = -fx' * B, where B is the baseline between the cameras.
Given a 3D point [X Y Z]', the projection (x, y) of the point onto the rectified image is given by:
This holds for both images of a stereo pair.

documentation: This method is deprecated. Please switch to GetRootAs.

methods.name: foxglove_schemas_flatbuffer.CompressedVideo.CompressedVideo.FrameId
documentation: Frame of reference for the video.
The origin of the frame is the optical center of the camera. +x points to the right in the video, +y points down, and +z points into the plane of the video.

methods.name: foxglove_schemas_flatbuffer.CompressedVideo.CompressedVideo.Data
documentation: For packet-based video codecs this data must begin and end on packet boundaries (no partial packets), and must contain enough video packets to decode exactly one image (either a keyframe or delta frame). Note: Foxglove does not support video streams that include B frames because they require lookahead.
Specifically, the requirements for different `format` values are:
- Each CompressedVideo message should contain enough NAL units to decode exactly one video frame
- Each message containing a key frame (IDR) must also include a SPS NAL unit
- Each CompressedVideo message should contain enough NAL units to decode exactly one video frame
- Each message containing a key frame (IRAP) must also include relevant VPS/SPS/PPS NAL units
- Each CompressedVideo message should contain exactly one video frame
- Each CompressedVideo message should contain enough OBUs to decode exactly one video frame
- Each message containing a key frame must also include a Sequence Header OBU

methods.name: foxglove_schemas_flatbuffer.CompressedVideo.CompressedVideo.Format
documentation: Note: compressed video support is subject to hardware limitations and patent licensing, so not all encodings may be supported on all platforms. See more about [H.265 support](https://caniuse.com/hevc), [VP9 support](https://caniuse.com/webm), and [AV1 support](https://caniuse.com/av1).
A single frame of a compressed video bitstream

methods.name: foxglove_schemas_flatbuffer.LinePrimitive.LinePrimitive.Color
documentation: Solid color to use for the whole line. One of `color` or `colors` must be provided.

methods.name: foxglove_schemas_flatbuffer.LinePrimitive.LinePrimitive.Colors
documentation: Per-point colors (if specified, must have the same length as `points`). One of `color` or `colors` must be provided.

methods.name: foxglove_schemas_flatbuffer.LinePrimitive.LinePrimitive.Indices
documentation: Indices into the `points` and `colors` attribute arrays, which can be used to avoid duplicating attribute data.
If omitted or empty, indexing will not be used. This default behavior is equivalent to specifying [0, 1, ..., N-1] for the indices (where N is the number of `points` provided).
A primitive representing a series of points connected by lines

methods.name: foxglove_schemas_flatbuffer.PointCloud.PointCloud.Pose
documentation: The origin of the point cloud relative to the frame of reference

methods.name: foxglove_schemas_flatbuffer.PointCloud.PointCloud.PointStride
documentation: Number of bytes between points in the `data`

methods.name: foxglove_schemas_flatbuffer.PointCloud.PointCloud.Fields
documentation: Fields in `data`. At least 2 coordinate fields from `x`, `y`, and `z` are required for each point's position; `red`, `green`, `blue`, and `alpha` are optional for customizing each point's color.
A collection of N-dimensional points, which may contain additional fields with information like normals, intensity, etc.

methods.name: foxglove_schemas_flatbuffer.CompressedImage.CompressedImage.FrameId
documentation: Frame of reference for the image. The origin of the frame is the optical center of the camera. +x points to the right in the image, +y points down, and +z points into the plane of the image.

methods.name: foxglove_schemas_flatbuffer.CompressedImage.CompressedImage.Format
documentation: Supported values: image media types supported by Chrome, such as `webp`, `jpeg`, `png`

documentation: Vertices to use for triangles, interpreted as a list of triples (0-1-2, 3-4-5, ...)

methods.name: foxglove_schemas_flatbuffer.TriangleListPrimitive.TriangleListPrimitive.Color
documentation: Solid color to use for the whole shape. One of `color` or `colors` must be provided.

methods.name: foxglove_schemas_flatbuffer.TriangleListPrimitive.TriangleListPrimitive.Colors
documentation: Per-vertex colors (if specified, must have the same length as `points`). One of `color` or `colors` must be provided.

methods.name: foxglove_schemas_flatbuffer.TriangleListPrimitive.TriangleListPrimitive.Indices
documentation: Indices into the `points` and `colors` attribute arrays, which can be used to avoid duplicating attribute data.
If omitted or empty, indexing will not be used. This default behavior is equivalent to specifying [0, 1, ..., N-1] for the indices (where N is the number of `points` provided).
A primitive representing a set of triangles or a surface tiled by triangles

modules.name: foxglove_schemas_flatbuffer.SceneEntity

documentation: This method is deprecated. Please switch to GetRootAs.

methods.name: foxglove_schemas_flatbuffer.SceneEntity.SceneEntity.Id
documentation: Identifier for the entity. A entity will replace any prior entity on the same topic with the same `id`.

methods.name: foxglove_schemas_flatbuffer.SceneEntity.SceneEntity.Lifetime
documentation: Length of time (relative to `timestamp`) after which the entity should be automatically removed. Zero value indicates the entity should remain visible until it is replaced or deleted.

methods.name: foxglove_schemas_flatbuffer.SceneEntity.SceneEntity.FrameLocked
documentation: Whether the entity should keep its location in the fixed frame (false) or follow the frame specified in `frame_id` as it moves relative to the fixed frame (true)

methods.name: foxglove_schemas_flatbuffer.SceneEntity.SceneEntity.Metadata
documentation: Additional user-provided metadata associated with the entity. Keys must be unique.
A visual element in a 3D scene. An entity may be composed of multiple primitives which all share the same frame of reference.
